main_event: Radar at Naha AB, Okinawa tracked unidentified object for 15 minutes on 7 February 1953 at 0122Z; F-94 scrambled, crew obtained visual of bright orange object changing to red/green, chased for ~15 minutes before breaking off due to fuel; investigation concluded visual was Venus (azimuth 273°, 10° above horizon) and radar tracks were false/spurious returns.
sighted_object: bright orange changing to intermingled red and green colors; pulled away from F-94 at high speed; no physical evidence; radar: 3 electronic plots over 15 minutes
witness_description: F-94 crew (pilot and RO); ground radar operators at Naha AB; number of individuals not specified
conclusion: Was Astronomical: Venus (visual) / False Radar Targets (radar)
interesting_points: Dual conclusion: visual assessed as Venus, radar as spurious false targets; no strict correlation between electronics and visual
Venus at azimuth 273°, elevation 10° above horizon at time of sighting — extremely bright period; F-94 vectored to Venus position
Red/green color change consistent with Venus observed through atmospheric layers at low elevation angle
F-94 broke off chase due to fuel shortage, not inability to close — object appeared to pull away at high speed
F-94 crew noted CCAF (Chinese Communist Air Force) aircraft in area — possible radar clutter source
Three radar plots obtained; correction/amendment message (C-2 priority) followed original teletype, suggesting initial report had errors
Record card duration field appears to read '9 days 15 min' — likely misread; actual radar track was 15 minutes per investigation report
